observations.recommendation: 2. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS) and supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipality within five days after the end of each month. In turn, within ten days from the receipts of such BS, the same should be reconciled with the General Ledgers which shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS must be regularly submitted to the Auditor with Bank Statements and/or Snapshots within ten days after the end of each month together with the paid checks and debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary journal entry voucher (JEVs) to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.
3. The Inventory Committee to conduct thorough review and physical count of the inventories and PPE by establishing its actual conditions and whereabouts, and come up with the RPCI and RPCPPE;

4. The Inventory Committee to adhere with the submission of RPCI for the first and second semesters not later than July 31 and January 31, respectively, and the annual RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to the Audit Team for subsequent review;

5. The Supply and Property Unit and Accounting Office to strengthen its system of maintaining its accounting and property records such as SCs, PCs, SLCs, PPELCs, RPCI and RPCPPE to facilitate periodic reconciliation of these records to ensure reliability of the inventories and PPE account balances as presented in the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

6. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts which its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in FS.

12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive annually submit the list of PPAs of the Municipality, at the beginning of the year; and prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

14. We recommended that Management should require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and furnished a copy to the Audit Team for review and verification. Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the GIF of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

23. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.

27. Submit/furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader with copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, and reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the project.

1.8 We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS) and supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipality within five (5) days after the end of each month. In turn, within 10 days from the receipts of such BS, the same should be reconciled with the General Ledgers which shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS must be regularly submitted to the Auditor with Bank Statements and/or Snapshots within 10 days after the end of each month together with the paid checks and debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary JEVs to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

2.5 We reiterated this recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list of taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card and make an effort to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable; adjust the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to present a more reliable and accurate balance.

3.11 We recommended that Management instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct an actual physical count of all its PPE by listing all the PPE items whether included in the latest Report on Physical Count of PPE/ Physical Inventory Report or not and compare it to all PPE Ledger Cards. This will be the basis in preparing the Physical Inventory Plan. Reconcile the balances derived by the Inventory Committee Report with the Accounting Records and take appropriate actions depending on the status of the PPE. Further, apply the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 or the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies.

4.7 We recommended that the Property Custodian regularly prepare/renew/update the AREs of the PPEs every three years or as soon as there is a change in custody of accountability to strengthen control and accountability over the government assets.
5.10 We therefore recommended that Management conduct an extensive review and adjustment of the PPE and Inventory Accounts of the Agency and adopt the provisions of the COA Circular 2022-004.

6.5 We recommended that Management instruct the AOs to (a) maintain the records and registers as cited under the Revised Cash Examination Manual and as presented in the Volume II of MNGAS for LGUs for more effective and efficient monitoring and control of funds and for proper accounting and auditing of related accounts thereafter in lieu of the cashbooks being maintained; and (b) to record all transactions of cash in bank including receipts and issuances of checks, record cancelled checks and debit and credit memos in the checkbooks.

7.4 We therefore recommended that the Accountable Officer prepare and regularly submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms with and without money value to the Audit Team using the format provided in Chapter 2, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs to ensure compliance with the afore-cited regulation and to facilitate inventory of accountable forms.

8.9 We reiterate this recommendation that Management notify the Audit Team at least three (3) days before there is an acquisition, delivery, inspection and acceptance activities of the agency and to submit the necessary inventory reports to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

9.7 We recommended and Management agreed to follow the provisions of COA Circular 2021-001 pertaining to the proper usage of RER and Certification in relation to reimbursement of expenses not requiring ORs with the proper guidance of COA Circular 2012-001 Prescribing the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions.

10.5 We recommended that Management instruct the BAC and Procurement Support Unit to stop the practice of submitting the contract and supporting documents together with the related disbursement vouchers, instead furnished the Auditor copy of the contract and other supporting documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

11.4 We recommended that Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose and as required under Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

12.5 We recommended that Management:
a. Submit to the Auditor’s Office a complete list of all on-going projects, programs and activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and should be updated every quarter following the format prescribed by COA Circular 2013-004, dated January 13, 2014 at the start of the year; and
b. Maintain the project status in Annex A of COA Cirular 2013-004 as current as possible and update using the schedule stated above based on the data sourced from monthly monitoring report prepared by the agency and verified by the Technical Audit Specialist of the Commission. .
13.6 We recommended that Management publicize not only the infrastructure PPAs but also the non-infrastructure pursuant to COA Circular 2016-003.

14.5 We therefore recommended that Management conduct planning conferences to ensure that all the buildings and other structures of the Agency are friendly to differently-abled persons/PWDs, and include the same in its budget pursuant to BP Blg 344 in order to address their rights to participate fully in the social life and the development of the societies within the Agency.

15.8 We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70 and ensure the quality of performance and compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

16.8 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and furnished a copy to the Audit Team for review and verification. Further, we recommended that Management insure all insurable assets with the GIF of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to RA 656.

17.8 We recommended that the Management prepare the PIF on or before April 30 of each that could be extracted from RPCPPE and RPCI and submitted the same to the Audit Team and GSIS to secure the inclusion in the GIF.

19.7 We recommended that the LGU establish GAD database to contain sex disaggregated data to serve as source of information for the conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

1. We recommended that the Accountable Officers conduct periodic monitoring submission of required reports to COA, Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and other regulatory bodies and see to it that all required reports are submitted on time.

7. We recommended that the management mark all motor vehicles of the Municipality with “For Official Use Only” and to exert an effort that existing motor vehicles which are not yet government plated be registered accordingly under place the name of the Municipality.

8. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list of taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card and make an effort to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
9. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable
observations.managementAction: adjust the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to present a more reliable and accurate balance.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, direct the Municipal Accountant to timely submit the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements and related supporting documents to the source agencies to avoid non-reconciliation of balances as well as to avoid the risk that they may not be able to be given additional funding from the National Government.

11. We therefore recommended that the management to include the Audit Team in the acquisition, delivery, inspection and acceptance activities of the agency and to submit the necessary inventory reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

13. We recommended that the management should instruct the HRMO Department to submit current plantilla of personnel every year and furnish the Audit Team copies of agency personnel movements (i.e. approved appointment/contract of service of every newly hired, promoted as well as the newly renewed contract) beforehand.

15. Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the corresponding AR to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014;
16. Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of disbursements.

17. Furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office of the Auditor and ensure that the utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 and refrain from appropriating or including in the appropriation of development projects the personnel services, not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

18. The Municipal Accountant obtain the necessary data pursuant from the Municipal Engineer, then prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for Local Road Network, provide the depreciation separately to each component of the road network, submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation, and disclose the road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2015-008;
19. The Municipal Mayor constitute a committee to conduct inventory of all road networks of the Municipality
20. The Municipal Mayor tap other qualified personnel to fill-in the shoe of a regular GSO or create the position of GSO if funds warrant it
21. The Municipal Engineer provides the local accountant and the Supply Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

22. We recommended the LCE require the Budget Officer to maintain the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure, as prescribed, to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

24. The Municipal Mayor should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with GSIS on the Interest & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long term outstanding premiums.
25. The LGU should maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund and 5% LDRRMF.

26. The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary records for each of the 31 barangays where undated shares from Real Property Taxes shall be recorded to facilitate the timely release of the same in compliance with Section 271 (d) of RA 7160.This will also serve as a tool in informing top management of the share of each barangay at any period of time.

28. Secure a copy of the Torrens Title Certificate and update the entries in the Subsidiary Ledgers and other accounting records. If no record is found, ownership of the land should be researched and donation should be requested from the owner of the property.

10. We recommended the LCE require the Budget Officer to maintain the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure, as prescribed, to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

14. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.

17. The Municipal Assessors should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.

19. Submit/furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader with copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the project.
21. We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We recommended that the Accountable Officers conduct periodic monitoring
of submission of required reports to COA, Department of Budget and Management
(DBM) and other regulatory bodies and see to it that all the required reports are
submitted on time.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository
banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS)
and supporting documents for all depository accounts maintained by the
Municipality within five days after the end of each month. In turn, within ten days
from the receipts of such BS, the same should be reconciled with the General
Ledgers which shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and henceforth, the BRS
must be regularly submitted to the Auditor with Bank Statements and/or Snapshots
within ten days after the end of each month together with the paid checks and
debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the necessary journal entry voucher (JEVs)
to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

We recommended the Management to require:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct thorough review and physical count of
the inventories and PPE by establishing its actual conditions and
whereabouts, and come up with the RPCI and RPCPPE;
b. The Inventory Committee to adhere with the submission of RPCI for the first
and second semesters not later than July 31 and January 31, respectively,
and the annual RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to the Audit
Team for subsequent review;
c. The Supply and Property Unit and Accounting Office to strengthen its
system of maintaining its accounting and property records such as SCs, PCs,
SLCs, PPELCs, RPCI and RPCPPE to facilitate periodic reconciliation of
these records to ensure reliability of the inventories and PPE account
balances as presented in the Financial Statements of the Municipality.
d. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for
the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer
should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated
Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus
facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the
basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation
expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its
appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to
present fairly the PPE accounts which its corresponding Accumulated
Depreciation Expense Accounts in FS.

We recommended that the management mark all motor vehicles of the
Municipality with “For Official Use Only” and to exert an effort that existing motor
vehicles which are not yet government plated be registered accordingly under which
place the name of the Municipality.

We reiterate this recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish at the year a certified list of
taxpayers with amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal
Accountant based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s
Index Card and make an effort to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use this as a basis in recording Real
Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable; adjust the
Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax to present a more reliable and
accurate balance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, direct the Municipal
Accountant to timely submit the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements and
related supporting documents to the source agencies to avoid non-reconciliation of
balances as well as to avoid the risk that they may not be able to be given additional
funding from the National Government.

We therefore recommended that the management to include the Audit Team
in the acquisition, delivery, inspection and acceptance activities of the agency and
the submit the necessary inventory reports to the Office of the Auditor within the
reglementary period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the personnel
concerned to submit the list of PPAs of the Municipality, annually at the beginning
of the year; and prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure
projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that the Management should instruct the HRMO
Department to submit current plantilla of personnel every year and furnish the
Audit Team copies of agency personnel movements (i.e. approved
appointment/contract of service of every newly hired, promoted as well as the newly
renewed contract) beforehand.

We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS and furnished a copy to the
Audit Team for review and verification.

Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to
fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656,
otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July
13, 1973.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for
review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the
corresponding AR to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period
provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and
b) Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with
DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of
disbursements.

Furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office of the Auditor and ensure
that the utilization of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1
and refrain from appropriating or including in the appropriation of development
projects the personnel services, not allowable to be charged to the 20% DF.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account in the general fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities for easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the funds pursuant to Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular No. 125 and Section 141 of the GAM for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommend that Management submit the distribution lists for verification and validation purposes.

We recommended that:
8. The LGU adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchase of supplies and materials.
9. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer prepare and maintain records and reports as required by the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
11. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the guidelines pertaining to the submission of and disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports.
12. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.
13. Demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transaction and to submit the same together with its supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant within the time prescribed by the regulations.

15. The Municipal Mayor should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the Interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long term outstanding premiums.
16. The LGU should maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund and 5% LDRRMF.

18. The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary records for each of the 31 barangays where undated shares from Real Property Taxes shall be recorded to facilitate the timely release of the same in compliance with Section 271 (d) of RA 7160. This will also serve as a tool in informing top management of the share of each barangay at any period of time.

20. Require the HRMO to conduct periodic review and render report to the Local Chief Executive on the workload assigned specifically to casual/contractual employees and consultants and assessment on the necessity of their continuance with the LGU.
22. The LGU should procure commonly used goods, supplies and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance with Administrative Order No. 17, series of 2011.

23. Secure a copy of the Torrens Title Certificate and update the entries in the Subsidiary Ledgers and other accounting records. If no record is found, ownership of the land should be researched and donation should be requested from the owner of the property.

We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated

We advise the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply
with the provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006. Ensure that the accounts together

road projects.

barangays where undated shares from Real Property Taxes shall be recorded to
facilitate the timely release of the same in compliance with Section 271 (d) of RA
7160. This will also serve as a tool in informing top management of the share of
each barangay at any period of time.

Require the Budget Officer to maintain the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment
and Obligation for each class of expenditure, as prescribed, to ensure the
preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a
given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of
expenses.

Require the concerned officials to submit/furnish the Office of the Audit Team
Leader with copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting
documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal
and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the
inspection of the projects.

Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with
adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide
the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.

Require the HRMO to conduct periodic review and render report to the Local Chief Executive on the workload assigned specifically to casual/ contractual employees and consultants and assessment on the necessity of the continuance of their employment with the LGU.

We advise the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly com-ply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006. Ensure that the accounts together with the corresponding reports be regularly submitted within the time frame to the Auditor.

The Municipal Mayor should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums.

The Budget Officer should maintain the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of expenditure as prescribed to ensure the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary records for each of the 31 barangays where undated shares from Real Property Taxes shall be recorded to facilitate the timely release of the same in compliance with Section 271 (d) of RA 7160. This will also serve as a tool in informing top management of the share of each barangay at any period of time.

The Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of a certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.
The LGU should maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund and 5% LDRRMF.

Instruct the concerned officials to submit/furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader with copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the projects.
We recommend that the LGU adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.
The LGU should procure commonly used goods, supplies and materials from the Procurement Service in compliance with Administrative Order No. 17, series of 2011.

The management should require every department or end-user unit of the LGU to prepare Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for their different programs, activities, and projects (PAPs) for consolidation into APP by the BAC.

Secure a copy of the Torrens Title Certificate and update the entries in the Subsidiary Ledgers and accounting records. If no record is found, ownership of the land should be researched and donation should be requested from the owner of the property.

Recommendation:
The Municipal Mayor should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of
unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest
due & surcharges. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to
remit the long outstanding premiums.

Recommendation:
The LGU should maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the 20%
Development Fund and 5% LDRRMF.

up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual,

observations.recommendation:
Require the LGU to  
esta-blish Local    
Disaster Risk       
Reduction and       
Manage-ment Office  
(LDRRMO) in the     
municipality and a  
Barangay Disaster   
Risk Reduction and  
Manage-ment Office  
(BDRRMO) in every   
barangay which shall
be responsible for  
setting the         
direction,          
development,        
implementation and  
coordi-nation of    
disaster risk       
management programs 
within their        
territorial         
jurisdiction.
